page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.i2gne.8xlarge is a storage-focused virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 32 dedicated physical cores on an x86_64 architecture, utilizing Intel Xeon 8163 processors running at a base frequency of 2.5 GHz. The system is equipped with 128 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. A key hardware feature is the inclusion of 3839 GB of local SSD storage, alongside a baseline network bandwidth of 10 Gbps.

Benchmark evaluations indicate a clear distinction between single-core and multi-core performance. Single-core metrics, such as stress-ng and Geekbench single-core tests, fall into the lower performance tiers, with single-threaded gzip compression showing poor throughput. However, multi-core benchmarks, including stress-ng multi-core, Passmark CPU, and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression, consistently achieve average tier performance. Memory bandwidth tests for both small cached blocks and large uncached blocks also align with mid-range performance standards.

Given the substantial 3839 GB of bundled local SSD storage and average multi-core processing capabilities, this instance offers high resource density for storage-heavy workloads without requiring external block storage attachments. This makes it qualitatively cost-efficient for applications requiring high local capacity and moderate compute, such as relational databases, NoSQL databases, and local caching layers like Redis, where it achieves average transaction rates. It is less suited for single-threaded tasks or compute-bound workloads requiring high-frequency single-core execution.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.i2gne.8xlarge is an x86_64 virtual server designed for storage-intensive workloads. Powered by the KVM hypervisor, it features 32 dedicated Intel Xeon 8163 physical cores operating at 2.5 GHz, 128 GB of RAM, and a substantial 3839 GB of bundled local SSD storage. The instance delivers 10 Gbps of baseline network bandwidth. Performance benchmarks show a contrast between weak single-core execution and average multi-core capabilities, with mid-range results in memory bandwidth, database operations, and multi-threaded processing. This resource profile provides qualitative cost efficiency for deployments requiring high local storage density alongside balanced memory-to-CPU ratios. It is best suited for database hosting, caching layers, and general-purpose workloads that benefit from large local storage capacities rather than high single-threaded compute performance.

tagline: A storage-dense virtual server featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors and substantial local SSD capacity for balanced database and caching workloads.

128 GB of system memory paired with 3839 GB of bundled local SSD storage
10 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on KVM hypervisor
Average multi-core and memory bandwidth benchmark performance
Optimal for database hosting, Redis caching, and storage-heavy workloads
